title: Returning the Wrong Size
summary: A customer brings a jacket back to a Toronto clothes shop because it is one size too small and asks the assistant about exchanging or refunding it.

body.speaker: Customer
body.text: Hi there. I bought this jacket here on Saturday, but I'd like to return it. It's a little too small.

body.speaker: Assistant
body.text: No problem. Have you got the receipt with you?

body.speaker: Customer
body.text: Yes, here it is. I tried it on at home and the sleeves are too tight when I move my arms.

body.speaker: Assistant
body.text: Let me see. You bought a size small. We can try a medium if you'd like, or you can have a refund.

body.speaker: Customer
body.text: I do love the colour. Could I try the medium first, please?

body.speaker: Assistant
body.text: Sure. The fitting rooms are just behind you. I'll bring one over in a moment.

body.speaker: Customer
body.text: Thank you. Do you have it in the same dark green?

body.speaker: Assistant
body.text: We have green and navy in medium. The dark green sold out quickly last weekend, but I think we still have one in the back.

body.speaker: Customer
body.text: Great. If the dark green fits, I'd love to keep it.

body.speaker: Assistant
body.text: Here you go. Take your time. Just call me if you need a different size.

body.speaker: Customer
body.text: Actually, the medium fits perfectly. The sleeves are just right and I can move easily.

body.speaker: Assistant
body.text: Looks lovely on you. Since the price is the same, we can do a straight exchange. No refund needed.

body.speaker: Customer
body.text: Perfect. Do I get a new receipt?

body.speaker: Assistant
body.text: Yes, I'll print one for you. Please keep it for thirty days, in case you change your mind.

annotations.exampleEn: Can I return it if it doesn't fit?
Please keep the receipt for returns.
I tried it on at home and it was too long.
These shoes are too tight on my feet.
The fitting rooms are next to the cashier.
The blue shirts were sold out by noon.
This dress fits perfectly on you.

annotations.meaningVi: đổi trực tiếp (không cần hoàn tiền)
annotations.exampleEn: Because the prices match, we can do a straight exchange.
If you change your mind, come back this week.
I prefer the navy version of the jacket.

grammarNotes.title: 'too' + adjective for excess
grammarNotes.bodyVi: 'too + adjective' diễn tả mức độ vượt quá mong muốn — quá nhỏ, quá chật, quá lớn… Trong bài: 'It's a little too small' và 'the sleeves are too tight'. Lưu ý khác với 'very' (rất). 'Too' luôn mang nghĩa tiêu cực.
grammarNotes.bodyEn: Use 'too + adjective' to say something is more than is acceptable. From the dialogue: 'It's a little too small' and 'the sleeves are too tight'. 'Too' has a negative meaning, unlike 'very'.

grammarNotes.title: First conditional ('if + present, will/can')
grammarNotes.bodyVi: Câu điều kiện loại 1 diễn tả tình huống có khả năng xảy ra trong tương lai: If + hiện tại đơn, … will / can / might + V. Trong bài: 'If the dark green fits, I'd love to keep it.' (here 'd love = would love — vẫn hợp với A2 như cách diễn đạt mong muốn).
grammarNotes.bodyEn: First conditional describes a likely future situation: If + present simple, ... will/can/might + verb. From the dialogue: 'If the dark green fits, I'd love to keep it.' Here 'I'd love' is a polite way to say 'I would like'.

translationVi:
Customer: Chào chị. Tôi mua chiếc áo khoác này ở đây hôm thứ Bảy, nhưng tôi muốn trả lại. Nó hơi nhỏ một chút.
Assistant: Không vấn đề gì ạ. Chị có mang hoá đơn không?
Customer: Có, đây ạ. Tôi thử ở nhà thì phần tay áo bị bó khi cử động cánh tay.
Assistant: Để em xem nào. Chị mua size S. Mình có thể thử size M nếu chị muốn, hoặc chị có thể nhận lại tiền.
Customer: Tôi rất thích màu này. Em cho chị thử size M trước được không?
Assistant: Được ạ. Phòng thử đồ ngay phía sau chị. Em sẽ mang qua ngay.
Customer: Cảm ơn em. Có đúng màu xanh đậm này không?
Assistant: Mình còn xanh đậm và xanh hải quân ở size M. Màu xanh đậm bán hết khá nhanh hồi cuối tuần, nhưng em nghĩ trong kho vẫn còn một chiếc.
Customer: Tuyệt. Nếu màu xanh đậm vừa, chị muốn lấy luôn.
Assistant: Đây ạ. Chị cứ thoải mái. Cần cỡ khác thì gọi em nhé.
Customer: À, size M vừa rất đẹp. Tay áo vừa khít và chị cử động thoải mái.
Assistant: Trông rất hợp với chị. Vì giá như nhau, mình đổi trực tiếp luôn nhé, không cần hoàn tiền.
Customer: Tốt quá. Em có hoá đơn mới không?
Assistant: Có ạ, em in cho chị. Chị giữ hoá đơn trong vòng 30 ngày phòng khi đổi ý nhé.

questions.prompt: When did the customer buy the jacket?
questions.explanation: 'I bought this jacket here on Saturday.'
questions.hint: Listen for the day she mentions.

questions.prompt: What is the problem with the jacket?
options: It is a little too small
It has a hole in it
questions.hint: Listen for an issue about size, not look.

questions.prompt: Where exactly are the sleeves too tight?
options: When she puts the jacket on
questions.explanation: 'the sleeves are too tight when I move my arms.'

questions.prompt: What two options does the assistant offer?
options: Buy two more or get nothing
A medium size or a refund
A different shop or a credit note
Wait or come back another day
questions.explanation: 'We can try a medium if you'd like, or you can have a refund.'
questions.hint: Listen for two choices linked by 'or'.

questions.prompt: What colours are available in medium?
questions.explanation: 'We have green and navy in medium.'

questions.prompt: Why was the dark green hard to find?
options: It sold out quickly last weekend
It was sent to another shop
questions.explanation: 'The dark green sold out quickly last weekend.'
questions.hint: Listen for what happened over the weekend.

questions.explanation: 'The fitting rooms are just behind you.'

questions.prompt: What does the customer say after trying the medium?
questions.explanation: 'the medium fits perfectly. The sleeves are just right and I can move easily.'
questions.hint: Listen for her opinion after the change.

options: Because the shop has a no-refund policy
Because the price of the medium is the same as the small
Because the customer paid by card
questions.explanation: 'Since the price is the same, we can do a straight exchange.'
questions.hint: Listen for a comment about price.

questions.prompt: What does the assistant ask the customer to keep, and for how long?
options: The new jacket for seven days
The old receipt for sixty days
The new receipt for thirty days
The card slip for a year
questions.explanation: 'Please keep it for thirty days, in case you change your mind.'
questions.hint: Listen for a number of days at the very end.

questions.prompt: How would you describe the interaction overall?
questions.explanation: Both speakers are polite, the assistant offers solutions, and the problem is solved quickly.
questions.hint: Think about tone and progress of the conversation.

cloze.template:
Customer: Hi there. I bought this jacket here on Saturday, but I'd like to {{b1}} it. It's a little too small.
Assistant: No problem. Have you got the {{b2}} with you?
Customer: Yes, here it is. I tried it on at home and the sleeves are too {{b3}} when I move my arms.
Assistant: Let me see. You bought a size small. We can try a {{b4}} if you'd like, or you can have a refund.
Customer: I do love the colour. Could I try the medium first, please?
Assistant: Sure. The {{b5}} rooms are just behind you. I'll bring one over in a moment.
Customer: Thank you. Do you have it in the same dark {{b6}}?
Assistant: We have green and navy in medium. The dark green {{b7}} out quickly last weekend, but I think we still have one in the back.
Customer: Great. If the dark green fits, I'd love to keep it.
Assistant: Here you go. Take your time. Just call me if you need a different size.
Customer: Actually, the medium {{b8}} perfectly. The sleeves are just right and I can move easily.
Assistant: Looks lovely on you. Since the price is the same, we can do a straight {{b9}}. No refund needed.
Customer: Perfect. Do I get a new receipt?
Assistant: Yes, I'll print one for you. Please keep it for thirty days, in case you change your {{b10}}.

blanks.explanation: 'return' — to bring an item back to the shop.
'medium' — the next size up from small.
'sold' — 'sold out' means all items are gone.
'fits' — used about the right size of clothes.

criticalThinkingQuestion: Many shops let you return clothes if you change your mind. Do you think this is good for customers and shops, or does it encourage people to buy things they don't really need? Why?
